content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

One correctness regression remains: stdout-only exhaustion messages can still be misclassified as retryable `TransportFailure` in both process and tmux backends due to not passing raw stdout text into output-based classification.

## Amendments

1. Critical path still misses plain-stdout exhaustion detection. In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs`, both non-zero-exit paths pass `stdout_for_class` as `stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default()` (lines `~1562`, `~1575`, and `~1633`), and `src/adapters/tmux.rs` does the same at lines `~632-642`. If `extract_stdout_error()` fails (plain text on stdout, not JSON), `classify_exit_failure_with_output()` receives `stdout=""` and backends that emit `usage limit`, `quota exceeded`, etc. only to stdout are classified as `TransportFailure` instead of `BackendExhausted` and retried. Add bounded raw stdout tail fallback (similar to `STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT`) for classification when `extract_stdout_error` is `None`, and pass that to `classify_exit_failure_with_output()`.
   Rationale: Severity: high. This violates the acceptance intent (stdout-only exhaustion should degrade gracefully) and causes unnecessary retries/reduced availability handling. Regression coverage currently exists for JSON stdout exhaustion (`tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:2100`, `2159`, etc.) but not for plain-stdout-only exhaustion messages in either adapter.
